item.title: Gilead HIV Trial Cancellation
item.description: Gilead Sciences has terminated a Phase 2/3 clinical trial evaluating a long-acting oral regimen for HIV treatment and prevention. The decision follows the FDA's placement of a clinical hold on the study, which remains in effect. The cancellation represents a setback for the development of this convenient dosing option in the HIV therapeutic landscape. Long-acting oral antiretroviral therapy (specific MoA would depend on the drug combination, typically involving integrase strand transfer inhibitors and/or nucleoside reverse transcriptase inhibitors). Endpoints News, Fierce Biotech
